name: California

examFacts.notes: Administered by the California Department of Real Estate (DRE).

intro:
About half of California real estate exam-takers don't pass on their first attempt. Because there are no separate national and state portions, every question can blend general real estate principles with California-specific law, so memorizing national content alone won't be enough to pass.

California tests its own rules in ways a national course never touches. Its community property rules, Proposition 13 and 19, agency disclosures, the Real Estate Recovery Fund, earthquake and coastal-zone disclosures, and DRE enforcement powers all show up on the exam. None of that looks like the material a candidate in a neighboring state studies. A generic real estate practice test won't cut it. You need California-specific practice questions.

faq.q: How many questions are on the California real estate exam?
faq.a: The California Real Estate Salesperson Examination has 150 multiple-choice questions. The DRE administers it as a single unified test, with no separate national portion. You have three hours to complete it.

faq.q: What's the passing score for the California real estate exam?
faq.a: 70%, which is 105 of 150 questions correct. The DRE doesn't curve and there's no partial credit, so every question on your practice test matters.

faq.q: How much does the California real estate exam cost?
faq.a: The salesperson examination fee is $100 per attempt as of July 1, 2024. If you pass, the salesperson license fee is an additional $350, and the license is valid for four years before renewal.

faq.q: How long is the California real estate exam?
faq.a: Three hours (180 minutes) for the salesperson exam. The DRE administers it at testing centers across the state, and you'll find out whether you passed before you leave.

faq.q: Who administers the California real estate exam?
faq.a: The California Real Estate Salesperson Examination is administered directly by the California Department of Real Estate (DRE), not an outside testing vendor. You register and schedule your exam date through the DRE.

faq.q: Is the California real estate exam hard?
faq.a: The first-time pass rate hovers around 50%, which gives you a sense of the difficulty. The questions aren't tricky for the sake of being tricky; they're written to test whether you actually understand California real estate law and practice. Most people who fail aren't lazy; they underestimated how much California-specific material is on the test (community property, Prop 13/19, agency disclosures, the DRE's role) and over-prepared on national principles. Honest practice on a California-specific practice exam is the difference between passing on the first try and retaking the $100 exam.

faq.q: What's on the California real estate exam?
faq.a: The 150-question DRE exam covers seven areas: property ownership and land use controls (about 15% of questions); laws of agency and fiduciary duties (about 17%); valuation and market analysis (about 14%); financing (about 9%); transfer of property (about 8%); practice of real estate and mandated disclosures (about 25%); and contracts (about 12%). California-specific law is woven into nearly every area, particularly community property, Proposition 13 and 19, the Real Estate Recovery Fund, and DRE disciplinary procedures.

faq.q: What's the best way to prepare for the California real estate exam?
faq.a: After finishing your 135-hour pre-license course, the highest-leverage thing you can do is grind through California-specific practice questions with honest feedback on every miss. Generic real estate practice tests will help with national principles but won't catch you on California's unique rules. The RealReady app gives you the full bank of California questions, plus progress tracking and a missed-question mode so you can drill what you're weak on. Most users study 30-60 minutes a day for 2-4 weeks before sitting the DRE exam.

The 20 California practice questions on this page are free, no account or signup needed. Unlocking the rest of the California bank is a single in-app purchase you pay once and own, with no recurring charge and no time limit on access.

faq.q: Does RealReady cover the California real estate exam?
faq.a: Yes. RealReady has a dedicated California question bank built for the California real estate exam, covering the national principles every candidate sees plus the California law that trips people up, like community property rules, Proposition 13 and 19, and the Real Estate Recovery Fund.

faq.q: Is RealReady a real estate license course?
faq.a: No. RealReady is exam practice, not a pre-license course, so it doesn't count toward California's required pre-license education hours. Use it after or alongside your coursework to drill realistic practice questions and find your weak spots before exam day.
